query: Russia annual GDP growth rate recent quarters 2025 and Q1 2026, what is the trajectory and latest official figures from Rosstat and Trading Economics
answer:
I've completed the research on Russia's GDP growth trajectory. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**Latest official figure (Q1 2026):** Russia's GDP **contracted -0.2% year-on-year**, per Rosstat's final estimate — the **first annual decline since Q1 2023**.

**2025 quarterly trajectory (clear deceleration):**
- Q1 2025: +1.4%
- Q2 2025: +1.1%
- Q3 2025: +0.6% (weakest since 2023)
- → Q1 2026: **-0.2%** (contraction)

**Full-year context:** 2025 growth ~1.0%, a sharp fall from 4.1% (2023) and ~4.9% (2024).

**Trajectory:** Decisively downward — the post-2022 sanctions-rebound boom has fully faded. Drivers include military-spending crowding out investment, low oil prices, weaker gas exports, a strong ruble, and very tight monetary policy.

**Forecasts/downgrades:** Russia's Economy Ministry cut its 2026 forecast to **0.4%** (from 1.3%); Sberbank cut to 0.5–1.0%. Trading Economics projects ~1.5% (2027) and ~2.0% (2028).

**One nuance:** Monthly 2026 data suggest tentative stabilization after a weak Jan–Feb — the Economy Ministry estimated ~1.3% y-o-y GDP growth in April 2026, and a composite five-sector index recovered from ~-3% (Jan–Feb) to ~+2% (Mar–Apr).

Sources include Trading Economics (sourced to Rosstat), FocusEconomics, BOFIT (Bank of Finland), Statista, Worldometer, and Sberbank/Reuters reporting. Full structured data has been submitted.

key_facts: Russia GDP contracted 0.2% YoY in Q1 2026 (Rosstat final estimate) - first annual decline since Q1 2023.
2025 quarterly deceleration: Q1 +1.4%, Q2 +1.1%, Q3 +0.6% (weakest since 2023).
Full-year 2025 GDP growth was approximately 1.0%, down from ~4.1% (2023) and ~4.3-4.9% (2024).
Q1 2026 sector contractions: manufacturing -1.5%, professional/scientific/technical -6.1%, transport & storage -1.8%.
Russia's Economy Ministry downgraded 2026 full-year GDP forecast to 0.4% from an earlier 1.3%.
Sberbank cut its 2026 Russia GDP forecast to 0.5%-1.0% (from 1.0%-1.5%) after weak Q1.
Monthly 2026 data: composite index ~-3% in Jan-Feb improved to ~+2% in Mar-Apr; Econ Ministry estimated ~1.3% y-o-y GDP growth in April 2026, suggesting stabilization.
Trading Economics projects Russia GDP growth ~1.5% in 2027 and ~2.0% in 2028.
GDP Annual Growth Rate averaged 2.78% (1996-2026); high +12.1% (Q4 1999), low -11.2% (Q2 2009).
Slowdown drivers: military spending focus, low oil prices, weaker gas exports, strong ruble, tight monetary policy (2025 inflation ~5.6%).
